articles.title: F5 Patches Two Critical NGINX Open Source Flaws Enabling Remote Code Execution
articles.summary: F5 has released security updates to address two critical security flaws in NGINX Open Source that could be exploited to achieve code execution on affected systems. articles.title: USB worm spreads crypto-stealing malware via Windows shortcut files
articles.summary: Threat actors targeting cryptocurrency wallets have been distributing clipboard-stealing malware with self-spreading capabilities and using the Tor network to conceal communication. [...]

articles.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Claude Chat Abuse, NastyC2 npm Packages, Device-Code Phishing + 25 More Stories
articles.summary: The internet did not break this week. It got used exactly as designed, which is worse. Searches were siphoned through shady browser add-ons. AI chat links turned into malware delivery paths. macOS attacks ran in memory and left almost nothing behind. Cloud agents looked like helpers until attackers

articles.title: Microsoft Details Windows Clipper Malware Campaign Using USB LNK Worm and Tor-Based C2
articles.summary: Microsoft has disclosed details of a Windows-based cryptocurrency clipper campaign codenamed CryptoBandits that has targeted users since February 2026 with clipboard-intercepting malware with self-spreading capabilities and using the Tor anonymity network to hide communication. articles.title: Australian sugar producer works to restore operations as ransomware group claims attack
articles.summary: Mackay Sugar said it was "working urgently" to verify claims that a highly active ransomware group was behind a cyberattack that shut down harvesting and milling operations.

articles.title: Klue OAuth breach linked to 'Icarus' Salesforce data theft attacks
articles.summary: Market intelligence platform Klue suffered a OAuth breach that enabled the "Icarus" threat actors to steal Salesforce CRM data from multiple organizations in an ongoing extortion campaign. [...]

articles.title: INC Ransomware Emerges as Major RaaS Threat in 2026 with 830+ Victims Since 2023
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have charted the evolution of INC from an nascent ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) operation to one of the most prolific cybercrime groups in 2026, claiming no less than 830 victims since August 2023. articles.title: DragonForce Hackers Abuse Microsoft Teams Relays to Hide Backdoor.Turn C2 Traffic
articles.summary: Threat actors associated with the DragonForce ransomware have been observed using a custom Go-based remote access trojan (RAT) called Backdoor.Turn to conceal command-and-control (C2) traffic inside Microsoft Teams relay infrastructure. According to findings from Broadcom-owned Symantec and Carbon B

articles.title: Police cleans nearly 15,000 SocGholish-infected sites tied to Evil Corp
articles.summary: International law enforcement agencies cleaned nearly 15,000 malware-infected WordPress websites and took down more than 100 servers linked to the SocGholish botnet and the Evil Corp Russian cybercrime group. [...]

articles.title: ShapedPlugin update flow hacked to infect WordPress sites
articles.summary: Multiple WordPress plugins from ShapedPlugin were compromised in a supply chain attack that distributed infected releases to paying customers via the vendor's official update system. [...]

articles.title: FortiBleed leak exposes Fortinet VPN credentials for 73,000 devices.
articles.summary: A newly discovered data leak dubbed "FortiBleed" has exposed what appears to be a collection of Fortinet and FortiGate VPN credentials for 73,932 firewall URLs at organizations worldwide. [...]

articles.title: No Exploits Required
articles.summary: Four decades of incident response experience suggest that exploits are often the symptom, not the root cause, of today’s cybersecurity failures. The post No Exploits Required appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Apple fixes Beats Studio Buds flaw that let hackers spy on conversations
articles.summary: Apple has released security updates to patch a high-severity flaw affecting the Beats Studio Buds wireless earbuds that could allow attackers in Bluetooth range to spy on users' conversations. [...]

articles.summary: CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation. articles.title: F5 issues out-of-band patches for critical NGINX vulnerabilities
articles.summary: Cybersecurity company F5 has released out-of-band security updates to address multiple NGINX web server vulnerabilities, including two critical-severity flaws that could allow attackers to execute code on vulnerable systems. [...]

articles.title: Atlassian, Splunk Patch Critical Vulnerabilities
articles.summary: Splunk patched an OS command injection in AI Toolkit, while Atlassian fixed dozens of flaws in third-party dependencies. The post Atlassian, Splunk Patch Critical Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Rokarolla Banking Trojan Targets 200 Applications
articles.summary: The Android malware allows its operators to take control of infected devices and harvest sensitive information. The post Rokarolla Banking Trojan Targets 200 Applications appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Critical Command Execution Vulnerability Patched in Cisco ISE
articles.summary: Insufficient validation of user input allows an attacker to gain access to the underlying OS and elevate their privileges to root. The post Critical Command Execution Vulnerability Patched in Cisco ISE appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: F5 Patches Critical, High-Severity NGINX Vulnerabilities
articles.summary: Critical flaws in NGINX could allow remote, unauthenticated attackers to cause a restart and potentially execute arbitrary code. The post F5 Patches Critical, High-Severity NGINX Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Kodak Admits Data Breach After ShinyHunters Hack Claims
articles.summary: Kodak told SecurityWeek it believes there is no threat to its systems or operations as a result of the cybersecurity incident. The post Kodak Admits Data Breach After ShinyHunters Hack Claims appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Crypto Clipper Campaign Abuses Fake Reviews, AI Narrators, and VirusTotal Comments
articles.summary: An unknown threat actor has been observed leveraging paid or promoted posts on legitimate news websites to drum up buzz for their warez, according to new findings from Check Point Research. The threat actor also has at their disposal a dedicated WordPress phishing page that acts as the central hub,

articles.title: Hostile states behind three-quarters of attacks on Britain's critical infrastructure, cyber chief warns
articles.summary: NCSC CEO Richard Horne warned that “kinetic targeting in any conflict tomorrow will be based on intelligence gathered today” and that nation-state adversaries were “prepositioning” throughout British critical infrastructure.

articles.title: Microsoft Confirms RoguePlanet Defender Zero-Day, Says Patch is in Development
articles.summary: Microsoft has formally disclosed that it's working to release a patch to address a Defender zero-day codenamed RoguePlanet. articles.title: EU grants Ukraine access to cybersecurity reserve for major attacks
articles.summary: As Kyiv takes steps toward formal accession to the EU, the bloc is integrating Ukraine with its pool of pre-approved cybersecurity incident response companies.

articles.title: Junior Hacker Used Tailscale and OpenSSH to Keep Access After His C2 Went Offline
articles.summary: A French-speaking attacker broke into a small French automotive business, planted a keylogger, and stole banking and email credentials. Ordinary stuff, until one move near the end. articles.title: Webinar Today: How Modern Breaches Bypass MFA and Evade Detection
articles.summary: Attendees will learn how attackers evade conventional detection methods, why legacy MFA alone is no longer sufficient, and how organizations can strengthen their defenses. The post Webinar Today: How Modern Breaches Bypass MFA and Evade Detection appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Why Account Takeovers Are Rising and How to Stop Them
articles.summary: Account takeovers are rising as attackers bypass traditional defenses through phishing, session hijacking, and MFA fatigue. Specops Software explores how device trust and continuous verification help reduce account takeover risk. [...]

articles.title: Malicious JetBrains Plugins Steal AI API Keys as Chrome Extensions Capture Chatbot Chats
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a "coordinated malware campaign" on the JetBrains Marketplace that has published no less than 15 malicious plugins capable of exfiltrating artificial intelligence (AI) provider keys. articles.title: Microsoft Teams Relay Servers Abused in DragonForce Ransomware Attack
articles.summary: The attackers deployed a new Go-based backdoor that uses Microsoft Teams servers for command-and-control. The post Microsoft Teams Relay Servers Abused in DragonForce Ransomware Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: The Top 10 Attack Surface Exposures in 2026
articles.summary: Breaches don't always start with a zero-day. An exposed admin panel can get brute-forced, or credentials reused from a previous attack. articles.title: CISA orders feds to patch max severity Joomla plugin flaw by Friday
articles.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has ordered federal agencies to patch a maximum-severity flaw in the Widget Factory Joomla Content Editor (JCE) plugin that is being actively exploited in the wild. [...]

articles.title: Microsoft Working on Patch for ‘RoguePlanet’ Zero-Day
articles.summary: The public PoC code exploits a race condition in Microsoft Defender to spawn a command prompt with System privileges. The post Microsoft Working on Patch for ‘RoguePlanet’ Zero-Day appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Microsoft working on Defender patch for RoguePlanet zero-day
articles.summary: Microsoft confirmed that it's working on a security patch for a Defender zero-day vulnerability named "RoguePlanet," disclosed one week ago. [...]

articles.title: Chrome and Firefox Updated to Patch Critical, High-Severity Vulnerabilities
articles.summary: The browser updates address multiple memory safety bugs that could potentially lead to remote code execution. The post Chrome and Firefox Updated to Patch Critical, High-Severity Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Joomla, LiteSpeed Vulnerabilities Exploited in Attacks
articles.summary: The flaws allow attackers to execute arbitrary PHP code and gain root privileges on shared hosting servers. The post Joomla, LiteSpeed Vulnerabilities Exploited in Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Kodak confirms data breach claimed by ShinyHunters extortion gang
articles.summary: Kodak has confirmed that it's working with external cybersecurity experts to investigate a security breach after hackers gained access to some of the company's data. [...]

articles.title: GitHub dismissed security reports on flaws now exploited by supply-chain worm, researchers say
articles.summary: GitHub rejected two formal vulnerability reports identifying design flaws that researchers say are enabling variants of the Shai-Hulud supply-chain worm to infect and compromise hundreds of software packages and developer accounts worldwide.

articles.title: New Rokarolla Android malware targets 217 banking, crypto apps
articles.summary: A new Android banking trojan named Rokarolla is targeting 217 banking and cryptocurrency applications using an extensive set of 137 commands. [...]

articles.title: Google Vertex AI SDK Flaw Let Attackers Hijack Model Uploads via Bucket Squatting
articles.summary: A flaw in the Google Cloud Vertex AI SDK for Python let an attacker with no access to a victim's project hijack the victim's machine learning model upload and run code inside Google's serving infrastructure. Palo Alto Networks Unit 42, which found and reported the bug through Google's bug bounty pro

articles.title: Steam Workshop abused to spread malware via Wallpaper Engine app
articles.summary: Threat actors are abusing Steam Workshop, Valve's community hub for downloading game-related content, to push various malware hidden in wallpaper packages. [...]

articles.title: ClickFix Campaigns Expand Malware Delivery With New Loaders and Fake Update Lures
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged multiple ClickFix campaigns that deliver three malware loaders called BabaDeda Loader, Lorem Ipsum Loader, and Potemkin, per independent reports from Morphisec, BlueVoyant, and Huntress, respectively.
